query: South Korea arms export deals 2026, especially Poland K2 tank second executive contract, any deals expected July-August 2026 worth over $5 billion
answer:
## Summary

I've completed the research. Here's the most important finding:

**The question's premise contains a date error.** The Poland K2 tank **"second executive contract"** worth **~$6.5 billion** (180 tanks + 81 support vehicles) was **already signed on 1 August 2025**, not July–August 2026. It is a completed, well-documented deal—the largest single arms export contract in South Korean history at the time.

**Key findings on your actual question (deals expected July–August 2026 worth over $5 billion):**

- **No confirmed South Korean arms export deal worth over $5 billion is specifically announced for signing in July–August 2026.**
- The only plausible candidate is a **potential third K2 executive contract** (~210 K2PL tanks), which a July 2026 report suggests *could* be signed sometime in 2026 ("if signed this year"), but with **no confirmed July–August date and no officially disclosed value**.

**Supporting context:**
- The full Poland K2 program (~1,000 tanks) is structured across **6 production contracts**: Contract 1 (2022, complete), Contract 2 (Aug 2025, ~$6.5B, in progress), Contract 3 (proposed, ~210 K2PL), and Contracts 4–6 (undetailed).
- On **28 April 2026**, Hyundai Rotem signed a K2PL local-production/maintenance agreement with Bumar-Łabędy (industrial localization, not a new procurement).
- Hanwha signed a **~$4B Chunmoo missile** production contract with Poland on 29 December 2025 (below the $5B threshold).
- South Korea's 2025 defense exports were **$15.4B**, with DAPA projecting 2026 to be higher; active pursuits include Poland's Orka submarine program, Canada, Germany/UK talks, and Latin America.

Full structured findings with 13 sources and 10 data points have been submitted.

sources.title: Poland doubles down on South Korean tanks with $6.5 billion deal
sources.snippet: Polish MoND ordered 180 K2 Black Panther tanks with 81 accompanying vehicles from Hyundai Rotem; deal worth around $6.5 billion; contract signed today in Gliwice.

sources.title: Hyundai Rotem signs $6.5 billion agreement with Poland for second batch of K2 main battle tanks
sources.snippet: Signing took place Friday 1 August at Bumar-Labedy plant in Gliwice; 116 K2GF delivered 2026-2027, then 64 K2PL 2028-2030, 61 K2PL final-assembled in Poland.

sources.title: Poland orders more K2 MBTs, support vehicles
sources.snippet: Poland signed the second executive contract with Hyundai Rotem for 180 more K2 MBTs at Bumar-Labedy in Gliwice on 1 August.

sources.title: With $6.7 billion in new tanks and vehicles, it's 'Armor Week' in Poland
sources.snippet: Warsaw signed almost $6.7 billion in ground vehicle contracts; nearly all tied to the $6.5 billion Hyundai Rotem second tank deal signed Friday morning.

sources.title: South Korean tanks for Poland (Defence24 factory report)
sources.snippet: 1,000 tanks ordered across 6 production contracts; details three contracts including a proposed third (~210 K2PL) implementable 2026-2034 if signed this year.

sources.title: Hyundai Rotem signs deal to build K2 tanks in Poland
sources.snippet: Hyundai Rotem signed a local production and maintenance cooperation agreement with Poland's Bumar-Labedy for K2PL tanks and armored recovery vehicles (April 2026).

sources.title: Hyundai Rotem signs local production pact for K2 tanks in Poland
sources.snippet: April 28, 2026: Hyundai Rotem signed a contract with Bumar-Labedy to locally manufacture the Polish K2PL variant, core of the second implementation phase.

sources.title: Korea's Defense Exports Hit $15.4 Billion in 2025, Set to Grow Further
sources.snippet: DAPA: defense exports reached $15.4 billion in 2025; this year will be even better.

sources.title: Korea Defense Industry 2026: The $37B K-Arms Export Boom
sources.snippet: Top-4 defense firms had ~$69B undelivered backlogs as of late 2024; combined revenue forecast to exceed $37B in 2026; 2022 framework was ~$12B.

sources.title: Hanwha Aerospace signs Chunmoo rocket production contract with Poland
sources.snippet: ~$4 billion (PLN 14 billion) executive contract signed 29 December 2025 for supply of Chunmoo CGR-080 guided rockets; third executive agreement under the framework.

sources.title: South Korea to transfer submarine to Poland for free
sources.snippet: Seoul plans to provide its first-generation Jang Bogo-class submarine to Poland at no cost to support a domestic bid for Warsaw's next-gen submarine program.

sources.title: Hanwha Aerospace in arms export talks with Germany, UK
sources.snippet: Hanwha Aerospace reportedly in discussions with Germany, UK and other European countries on potential arms deals (May 2026).

sources.title: Poland signs $6.5 billion deal to purchase 2nd batch of K2 tanks
sources.snippet: Poland on Friday signed ~$6.5 billion deal for second batch of K2 tanks; largest-ever arms export contract by a South Korean company; first major one under Lee Jae Myung administration.

key_facts: The Poland K2 'second executive contract' (~$6.5 billion, 180 tanks + 81 support vehicles) was signed on 1 August 2025, NOT July-August 2026, contradicting the question's premise.
No confirmed South Korean arms export deal worth over $5 billion is specifically announced for signing in July-August 2026.
A potential third K2 executive contract (~210 K2PL tanks) is described as possibly being signed 'this year' (2026) per a July 2026 report, but has no confirmed date or official value.
The full Poland K2 program (~1,000 tanks) is structured across 6 production contracts; only contracts 1 (2022) and 2 (Aug 2025) are signed; contract 3 is proposed.
On 28 April 2026, Hyundai Rotem signed a K2PL local production/maintenance agreement with Poland's Bumar-Labedy (industrial localization, not a new procurement contract).
First contract's 180 K2GF tanks were fully delivered to Poland by end of 2025; Contract 2's first K2GF deliveries expected Q3 2026.
South Korea's 2025 defense exports reached $15.4 billion (DAPA), with 2026 projected to be higher.
Hanwha signed a ~$4 billion Chunmoo CGR-080 guided-missile production contract with Poland on 29 December 2025 (below $5B).
Top-4 Korean defense firms hold ~$69 billion in undelivered order backlogs; combined 2026 revenue forecast exceeds $37 billion.
South Korea is pursuing Poland's next-gen submarine (Orka) program, offering a free Jang Bogo-class submarine to support Hanwha's bid.
